project_name: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier. Sourced from EMPLOYEE_ID decimal(10,0) PK. Zero fractional digits warrant INT64 over NUMERIC.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Employee given name. Sourced from FIRST_NAME varchar(50). BigQuery STRING is unbounded; source length constraint remains at origin.

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Employee family name. Sourced from LAST_NAME varchar(50).

columns.name: email
columns.description: Corporate email address. Sourced from EMAIL varchar(100).

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Contact phone number. Sourced from PHONE_NUMBER varchar(20). Nullable in source; null-constraint violations must be logged per acceptance criteria.

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Date the employee was hired. Sourced from HIRE_DATE date. Used as TIME_UNIT partition column at MONTH granularity to support cohort analytics and partition pruning.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role code. Sourced from JOB_ID varchar(20). Cluster column (position 2) — frequent GROUP BY and WHERE target in HR reporting.

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee base salary with two decimal places. Sourced from SALARY decimal(8,2). BigQuery NUMERIC (29,9) safely accommodates this precision without rounding loss.

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage with two decimal places. Sourced from COMMISSION_PCT decimal(4,2). Nullable; null-constraint violations must be logged before load is marked complete.

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referential FK to employee_id identifying the employee's direct manager. Sourced from MANAGER_ID decimal(10,0). Nullable for top-level employees with no manager.

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier. Sourced from DEPARTMENT_ID decimal(4,0). Zero fractional digits warrant INT64. Cluster column (position 1) — highest-cardinality analytical dimension in HR queries.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status flag (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE). Sourced from STATUS varchar(10). Low-cardinality; cluster column (position 3) for efficient point-filter queries.

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Record creation timestamp. Sourced from CREATED_DATE timestamp. NOT NULL; used as fallback incremental watermark when updated_date is NULL.

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Record last-modified timestamp. Sourced from UPDATED_DATE timestamp. Primary incremental load watermark column for delta extraction. Null-constraint violations must be logged and reported before each incremental load is marked complete.

cardinality.notes: Many employees can share the same manager_id, which references employee_id within the same table forming a supervisory hierarchy. Top-level employees (no manager) carry NULL in manager_id.
